title: Poached Herbal Herbs Soup
description: This herbal, crunchy, cozy Mediterranean dish features treacle for sweetness without added sugar and is perfect as a light lunch or snack.

ingredients.name: 1 cup chopped fresh herbs (thyme, parsley, dill)
ingredients.notes: Choose your favorite herbs for this dish.

ingredients.name: 2 cups vegetable broth
ingredients.notes: Use low-sodium or make it from scratch if preferred.

ingredients.name: 1/4 cup treacle (unrefined molasses, can substitute with honey)
ingredients.notes: Choose a sugar-free version of treacle for the sugar-free-friendly option.

ingredients.name: Pinch salt and freshly ground black pepper
3 cloves garlic, minced
ingredients.notes: Enhances the flavor without making it overpowering.

ingredients.name: 1 small onion, finely chopped
ingredients.notes: Adds a savory note to the soup.

ingredients.name: 2 cups water or low-sodium vegetable broth
ingredients.notes: Provides the liquid base for poaching the herbs and treacle.

ingredients.name: Vegetable stock cube (optional)
ingredients.notes: For a richer, more complex flavor.

instructions: Combine water or vegetable broth with chopped fresh herbs, minced garlic, finely chopped onion, salt, pepper, and treacle in a pot.
Bring to a boil over medium-high heat. Reduce the heat to low and let simmer for about 20 minutes, ensuring the herbs are tender but still slightly crunchy.
If desired, use a vegetable stock cube for extra depth of flavor.
Strain the soup through a fine mesh strainer into a bowl, discarding solids. If you prefer your soup smoother, blend with an immersion blender or transfer to a blender and puree until smooth.
Serve hot as a light lunch or snack.
